title: anisomeris diversifolia
intro: Anisomeris diversifolia, also known as the Desert holly or Yerba de pasmo, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Yerba de pasmo is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
